title: The Canopy Congregation

features.name: unity-canopy
features.desc-on-touch: Here individual trees recover the unity they lost in the ravine below. You are between division and gathering, between the count that speaks and the hold that is silent.

features.name: congregation-gather
features.desc-on-touch: The canopy does not add—it holds. Each tree a voice, but the assembly is not sum. Something emerges in the silence between branches.

features.name: sky-band
features.desc-on-touch: The sky above shifts in bands—dusk into dark into growth into light—the gradient that holds all moments at once.

features.name: separate-silence
features.desc-on-touch: The overstory's separate counts fall silent here, not erased but held in a moment where individual means something different.

features.name: deep-holding
features.desc-on-touch: You feel the deep hold—not in words, not in numbers, but in the pressure of many things becoming one thing that is still many.

inscription: the canopy holds what the ravine divides; here unity is not sum but simultaneity

file.name: Overstory_Congregation_Silence_Field_Recording_Dawn.mp3
file.note: Where separate counts become one hold. The archive is learning the grammar of what holds without adding.

exits.label: back down to the overstory where the hold was first named
deeper into the canopy where the congregation learns new forms
toward the ravine's far edge where the separate counts begin again
